rulings.Case Title: FRANCES SUSSNA VS. PRIME OLD COUNTY LP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication To Plaintiffs Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, April 10, 2015, line 1. DEFENDANT KARI RILEY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication To Plaintiffs Complaint CONTINUED to May 7, 2014. Opposing Party's Separate Statement of Material Facts fails to clearly delineate which facts are disputed and which are undisputed, in violation of California Rule of Court 3.1350(f). (See Parkview Villas Ass'n Inc. v. State Farm Fire and Cas. Co. (2005) 133 Cal.App.4th 1197, 1211.) Opposing party shall file with the court an amended Separate Statement which complies with all the requirements of California Rule of Court 3.1350(f) by April 20, 2015. Defendant may then file an amended response, if it wishes, by April 27, 2015. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BARNABA BIENKOWSKI et al VS. LAWRENCE CHOY et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, April 10, 2015, line 2. DEFENDANT RICKY LAM'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT CONTINUED to April 23, 2015. Opposing Party's Separate Statement of Material Facts fails to cite to any evidence in support of its contentions that certain facts are disputed, in violation of California Rule of Court 3.1350. This deprives the Court of the ability to consider the Separate Statement. (See Parkview Villas Ass'n Inc. v. State Farm Fire and Cas. Co. (2005) 133 Cal.App.4th 1197, 1211.) Opposing party shall file with the court an amended Separate Statement which complies with all the requirements of California Rule of Court 3.1350(f) by April 16, 2015. Defendant may then file an amended response, if it wishes, by April 20, 2015. The Court finds good cause for rescheduling the hearing date within 30 days of trial under Code of Civil Procedure Section 437c(a). Failure to comply with CRC 3.1350 and the Court's tentative ruling will result in the motion being granted. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ELLEN TSANG VS. CHARLES ENGELBERG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; And Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, April 10, 2015, line 3. DEFENDANT CHARLES ENGELBERG'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; And Request For Judicial Notice GRANTED. Plaintiff's fourth cause of action, harassment in violation of California Code of Civil Procedure 527.6, is defective as appended to a civil complaint for damages. If Plaintiff wishes to pursue a civil restraining order against Defendant she must do so in the proper Department using the mandatory application forms and procedures which apply thereto. The Court therefore dismisses the cause of action, with leave to amend if Plaintiff can plead facts and law sufficient to state a cause of action for harassment under some theory of recover other than California Code of Civil Procedure 527.6. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN LEE VS. BYRON W HANCOCK
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And Accompanying Papers
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, April 10, 2015, line 6. Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And Accompanying Papers GRANTED. Plaintiff fails to carry his burden to show a probability of success on the merits. Most fundamentally, Plaintiff's theory of an oral contract later ratified by the counteroffer assumes that Morcos had authority to accept on behalf of the seller, yet Plaintiff fails to show that Morcos, the broker for the deal, had authority to accept on behalf of the sellers. Such an agreement must be in writing, signed by the owners, and clear, distinct, and certain in its meaning. (Lyne v. Bonner (1954) 129 Cal.App.2d 743, 746.) No such written agreement is submitted nor is its existence alleged. The most reasonable interpretation of the evidence presented is that the sellers responded to Plaintiff's offer with a counter-offer of their own, and Plaintiff did not accept this offer by the expiry of that offer. Accordingly, no contract was formed. Defendant may move for attorneys' fees and costs incurred in conjunction with this motion. = (501/REQ)
